title: Red Fort
tagline: Red Fort — heritage city of Delhi

overview.short: The Red Fort (Lal Qila in Hindi; Hindi pronunciation: [laːl 'qɪlaː]) is a historic Mughal fort located in the Old Delhi area of Delhi, India.
overview.description: The Red Fort (Lal Qila in Hindi; Hindi pronunciation: [laːl 'qɪlaː]) is a historic Mughal fort located in the Old Delhi area of Delhi, India. Serving as the main residence of the Mughal emperors, it was commissioned by Emperor Shah Jahan on the 12th of May 1639; the fort was constructed following his decision to shift the Mughal capital from Agra to Delhi. Originally adorned in red and white, the fort's design is attributed to Ustad Ahmad Lahori, the architect of the Taj Mahal. The Red Fort is a prominent example of Mughal architecture from Shah Jahan's reign, combining Persian and Indian arch

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Indian War Memorial Museum
topPlaces.description: The Indian War Memorial Museum is in the Naubat Khana of the Red Fort in Delhi, northern India. It was built in 1919 as a tribute to commemorate the soldiers who had joined the First World War in India or abroad on behalf of the British Empire.

topPlaces.name: Naubat Khana (Red Fort)
topPlaces.description: The Naubat Khana, or Naqqar Khana, is the drum house that stands at the entrance between the outer and inner court at the Red Fort in Old Delhi. It is believed by some historians to be the site of the assassinations of Mughal emperors Jahandar Shah and Farrukhsiyar in the early 18th century.

topPlaces.name: Chhatta Chowk
topPlaces.description: The Chhatta Chowk (transl. Covered Bazaar) is a long passage way that contains a bazaar, or market, located in the Red Fort of Old Delhi, India. The Chhatta Chowk is located behind the Lahori Gate and is set within an arched passage.

topPlaces.name: Lahori Gate, Red Fort, Delhi
topPlaces.description: The Lahori Gate is the main entrance to the Red Fort in Old Delhi. The fort is approached through a covered street flanked by arcaded apartments called the Chhatta Chowk.

topPlaces.name: Diwan-i-Am (Red Fort)
topPlaces.description: The Diwan-i-Am, or Hall of Audience, is a building in the Red Fort of Old Delhi where the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan (1592–1666) and his successors received members of the general public and heard their grievances. The inner main court to which the Nakkarkhana led was 540 feet broad, 420 feet deep, and surrounded by arcade galleries, where chieftains (umaras) on duty were posted.

topPlaces.name: Moti Masjid (Red Fort)
topPlaces.description: The Moti Masjid (lit. 'Pearl mosque') is a 17th-century historical mosque, not open for worship, inside the Red Fort complex in Old Delhi, India. It was built by Mughal emperor Aurangzeb, damaged during the Siege of Delhi, and subsequently restored by the British.

topPlaces.name: Hayat Bakhsh Bagh
topPlaces.description: The Hayat Baksh Bagh, which means "Life-bestowing garden", is the largest of the gardens in the Red Fort in Old Delhi . The North Eastern portion of the Red Fort.

topPlaces.name: Rang Mahal (Red Fort)
topPlaces.description: The Rang Mahal or Palace of Colour is located in the Red Fort, Old Delhi. It originally served as a part of the imperial harem.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Indian War Memorial Museum & around

items.note: The Indian War Memorial Museum is in the Naubat Khana of the Red Fort in Delhi, northern India. It was built in 1919 as a tribute to commemorate the soldiers who had joined the First World War in India or abroad on behalf of the British Empire.

The Naubat Khana, or Naqqar Khana, is the drum house that stands at the entrance between the outer and inner court at the Red Fort in Old Delhi. It is believed by some historians to be the site of the assassinations of Mughal emperors Jahandar Shah and Farrukhsiyar in the early 18th century.

The Chhatta Chowk (transl. Covered Bazaar) is a long passage way that contains a bazaar, or market, located in the Red Fort of Old Delhi, India. The Chhatta Chowk is located behind the Lahori Gate and is set within an arched passage.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Lahori Gate, Red Fort, Delhi & around

items.note: The Lahori Gate is the main entrance to the Red Fort in Old Delhi. The fort is approached through a covered street flanked by arcaded apartments called the Chhatta Chowk.

The Diwan-i-Am, or Hall of Audience, is a building in the Red Fort of Old Delhi where the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan (1592–1666) and his successors received members of the general public and heard their grievances. The inner main court to which the Nakkarkhana led was 540 feet broad, 420 feet deep, and surrounded by arcade galleries, where chieftains (umaras) on duty were posted.

The Moti Masjid (lit. 'Pearl mosque') is a 17th-century historical mosque, not open for worship, inside the Red Fort complex in Old Delhi, India. It was built by Mughal emperor Aurangzeb, damaged during the Siege of Delhi, and subsequently restored by the British.
The Hayat Baksh Bagh, which means "Life-bestowing garden", is the largest of the gardens in the Red Fort in Old Delhi . The North Eastern portion of the Red Fort.
The Rang Mahal or Palace of Colour is located in the Red Fort, Old Delhi. It originally served as a part of the imperial harem.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Red Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Red Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Red Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (18 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Red Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Red Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Red Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Red Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Red Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Indian War Memorial Museum, Naubat Khana (Red Fort), Chhatta Chowk, Lahori Gate, Red Fort, Delhi, Diwan-i-Am (Red Fort).
